notes: PDP authorization on every operation. Fail-closed if PDP unreachable. Per-operation granularity

Admin CLI can enable/disable keys. No built-in break-glass procedure; operational responsibility
Session management is a client/application concern, not KMS
AES-256-GCM for symmetric encryption. HSM-backed key storage. Encrypt/Decrypt APIs
Every operation emits audit events via NATS. Denied requests logged. Principal, action, resource, timestamp recorded

AES-GCM authenticated encryption. Encryption context binds ciphertext to its intended use via AAD
Bearer token and mTLS authentication. Constant-time token comparison (subtle crate)
gRPC/REST over TLS. PKCS#11 over local socket or TLS. Key material never transmitted in plaintext
TLS provides transmission integrity. AES-GCM authentication tag provides ciphertext integrity end-to-end
All API communication over TLS 1.2+. Plaintext ePHI encrypted by KeyRack before storage
Key states aligned to SP 800-57 (see nist-sp-800-57.csv). Full lifecycle management
NATS durable delivery for FOSS. Signed audit receipts in commercial edition. FOSS lacks cryptographic tamper-evidence
Key inventory, audit trail, and metrics available for assessment. No built-in assessment report generation
